title: date_part function return wrong result for infinite or -infinite timestamp
description:
### What happens?

When extracting the year from an 'infinity' timestamp, DuckDB returns NULL instead of the expected infinity integer value.


### To Reproduce

```
D SELECT date_part('year','infinity'::TIMESTAMP);
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ date_part('year', CAST('infinity' AS TIMESTAMP)) │
│                      int64                       │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       NULL                       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

```



### OS:

Mac OS

### DuckDB Version:

1.4.0 (Andium)

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Zhibai Song

### Affiliation:

AWS

key_bug_features: date_part function with 'infinity' timestamp returns NULL
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INT, event_date TIMESTAMP, description VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, 'infinity', 'Event with infinite date');
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (2, '2025-10-02', 'Regular event date');
SELECT date_part('year', event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 1;
SELECT date_part('year', event_date) FROM test_dates WHERE id = 2;
